name: Consistency Analyzer
description: Compares adapter-observed span I/O (ground truth) against node self-reported audit events (claims) per C007. For each span, extracts output field set from adapter observation and compares against the node's audit event field set. Unexplained fields (observed but not declared/claimed) produce INCONSISTENT findings with field details. All observed fields claimed = CONSISTENT. Missing claims produce MISSING_CLAIM findings (not exceptions). HIGH severity inconsistency during soak is a hard stop. Findings are persisted via FindingStore and queryable by node/span. Consistency outcomes feed into trust engine (consistency_factor updates). Provides findings list for report generation and CLI `arbiter findings`. Serialization uses JSONL with sorted field arrays, ISO 8601 UTC timestamps, and schema_version field.

types.name: NodeId
types.description: NewType wrapper over str identifying a unique node in the Pact/Baton circuit. Non-empty, typically dot-separated path.

types.name: SpanId
types.description: NewType wrapper over str identifying a unique OTLP span. Non-empty, hex-encoded 16-byte identifier.

types.name: TraceId
types.description: NewType wrapper over str identifying a unique OTLP trace. Non-empty, hex-encoded 32-byte identifier.

types.name: Timestamp
types.description: ISO 8601 UTC datetime string (e.g. '2024-01-15T12:00:00Z'). Always timezone-aware UTC.

types.name: FieldSet
types.description: A set of canonical dot-notation field names. Represented as frozenset[str] in Python. Serialized as a sorted JSON array.

types.name: ConsistencyOutcome
types.description: The outcome of comparing adapter observation against node audit claim for a single span.

types.name: FindingSeverity
types.description: Severity level for a consistency finding. Shared enum expected to live in a common module. NONE = no issue, LOW = minor overclaim only, MEDIUM = small unexplained set, HIGH = significant unexplained fields or missing claim.

types.name: AdapterObservation

fields.name: span_id
fields.description: The OTLP span identifier this observation covers.

fields.name: trace_id
fields.description: The OTLP trace identifier this span belongs to.

fields.name: node_id
fields.description: The circuit node that produced this span.

fields.name: observed_fields
fields.description: Canonical dot-notation field names observed in the span output by the adapter. May be empty (vacuous case).

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: UTC timestamp when the adapter captured this observation.

types.description: Frozen Pydantic v2 model representing the adapter's ground-truth observation of a span's I/O fields. The adapter layer is authoritative.

types.name: NodeAuditClaim

fields.name: span_id
fields.description: The OTLP span identifier this claim covers.

fields.name: trace_id
fields.description: The OTLP trace identifier this span belongs to.

fields.name: node_id
fields.description: The circuit node making this audit claim.

fields.name: claimed_fields
fields.description: Canonical dot-notation field names the node claims it accessed/produced. May be empty.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: UTC timestamp when the node emitted this audit event.

types.description: Frozen Pydantic v2 model representing a node's self-reported audit event declaring which fields it accessed or produced.

types.name: ConsistencyFinding

fields.name: schema_version
fields.description: Schema version for serialization compatibility. Always 1 for this contract version.

fields.name: node_id
fields.description: The node this finding pertains to.

fields.name: span_id
fields.description: The span this finding pertains to.

fields.name: trace_id
fields.description: The trace this finding pertains to.

fields.name: outcome
fields.description: The consistency verdict for this span comparison.

fields.name: severity
fields.description: Severity derived deterministically from outcome and field differences. NONE for CONSISTENT, HIGH for MISSING_CLAIM, LOW for overclaim-only, MEDIUM/HIGH for unexplained fields by count.

fields.name: observed_fields
fields.description: Fields the adapter observed (ground truth). Empty list if observation was absent (MISSING_OBSERVATION).

fields.name: claimed_fields
fields.description: Fields the node claimed. Empty list if claim was absent (MISSING_CLAIM).

fields.name: unexplained_fields
fields.description: Fields observed by adapter but NOT claimed by node: observed_fields - claimed_fields. Non-empty implies INCONSISTENT.

fields.name: overclaimed_fields
fields.description: Fields claimed by node but NOT observed by adapter: claimed_fields - observed_fields. Indicates node claims more than reality.

fields.name: analyzed_at
fields.description: UTC timestamp when this analysis was performed. Set by the analyzer at computation time.

fields.name: details
fields.description: Optional human-readable detail string providing additional context about the finding.

types.description: Frozen Pydantic v2 model representing the result of comparing one adapter observation against one node audit claim (or absence thereof) for a single span. This is the primary output artifact of consistency analysis.

types.name: OptionalNodeAuditClaim
types.description: A NodeAuditClaim or None. None indicates the node did not produce an audit event for this span (MISSING_CLAIM case).

types.name: OptionalAdapterObservation
types.description: An AdapterObservation or None. None indicates no adapter observation exists for this span (MISSING_OBSERVATION case).

types.name: AnalysisPair

fields.name: observation
fields.description: The adapter observation, or None if missing.

fields.name: claim
fields.description: The node audit claim, or None if missing.

types.description: A paired observation and claim for batch analysis. At least one of observation or claim must be non-None.

types.name: AnalysisPairList
types.description: A sequence of observation/claim pairs for batch analysis.

types.name: ConsistencyFindingList
types.description: An ordered list of ConsistencyFinding results.

types.name: OptionalTimestamp
types.description: An optional UTC timestamp. None means no time bound.

types.name: ConsistencyAnalysisError

fields.name: node_id
fields.description: The node_id associated with the error, or empty string if not applicable.

fields.name: span_id
fields.description: The span_id associated with the error, or empty string if not applicable.

fields.name: detail
fields.description: Human-readable description of what went wrong, including the specific field or domain that caused the error.

types.description: Exception raised for malformed or invalid inputs to analysis functions. Always includes context identifying the problematic node, span, or field.

functions.name: analyze_span
functions.description: Compares a single adapter observation against a single node audit claim for one span. If observation is present and claim is None, produces MISSING_CLAIM finding. If claim is present and observation is None, produces MISSING_OBSERVATION finding. If both present, computes set differences to determine CONSISTENT or INCONSISTENT outcome. Severity is derived deterministically from outcome and unexplained field count. Both None raises ConsistencyAnalysisError. When both present, span_id and node_id on observation and claim must match or ConsistencyAnalysisError is raised.

inputs.name: observation
inputs.description: Adapter ground-truth observation for this span, or None if no observation exists.

inputs.name: claim
inputs.description: Node self-reported audit claim for this span, or None if the node did not report.

error_cases.name: both_none
error_cases.condition: Both observation and claim are None

error_data.detail: analyze_span requires at least one of observation or claim; both were None

error_cases.name: span_id_mismatch
error_cases.condition: Both observation and claim are non-None but observation.span_id != claim.span_id

error_data.detail: span_id mismatch between observation ({observation.span_id}) and claim ({claim.span_id})

error_cases.name: node_id_mismatch
error_cases.condition: Both observation and claim are non-None but observation.node_id != claim.node_id

error_data.detail: node_id mismatch between observation ({observation.node_id}) and claim ({claim.node_id})

error_cases.name: trace_id_mismatch
error_cases.condition: Both observation and claim are non-None but observation.trace_id != claim.trace_id

error_data.detail: trace_id mismatch between observation ({observation.trace_id}) and claim ({claim.trace_id})

error_cases.name: malformed_observation
error_cases.condition: Observation contains invalid field names (not valid dot-notation identifiers)

error_data.detail: Malformed field name in observation for node {node_id}, span {span_id}: {field_name}

error_cases.name: malformed_claim
error_cases.condition: Claim contains invalid field names (not valid dot-notation identifiers)

error_data.detail: Malformed field name in claim for node {node_id}, span {span_id}: {field_name}

preconditions: At least one of observation or claim must be non-None
If both are non-None, observation.span_id == claim.span_id
If both are non-None, observation.node_id == claim.node_id
If both are non-None, observation.trace_id == claim.trace_id
All field names in observed_fields and claimed_fields are valid dot-notation identifiers
postconditions: result.outcome == MISSING_CLAIM if observation is not None and claim is None
result.outcome == MISSING_OBSERVATION if claim is not None and observation is None
result.outcome == CONSISTENT if both present and unexplained_fields is empty and overclaimed_fields is empty
result.outcome == INCONSISTENT if both present and (unexplained_fields is non-empty or overclaimed_fields is non-empty)
result.unexplained_fields == observed_fields - claimed_fields (as sets)
result.overclaimed_fields == claimed_fields - observed_fields (as sets)
result.severity == NONE when outcome == CONSISTENT
result.severity == HIGH when outcome == MISSING_CLAIM
result.analyzed_at is a valid UTC timestamp at or after invocation time
result.schema_version == 1

structured_side_effects.description: Pure computation; no side effects. Caller is responsible for persisting the returned finding.

functions.name: analyze_batch
functions.description: Analyzes a sequence of observation/claim pairs, returning one ConsistencyFinding per pair. Each pair is analyzed independently using the same logic as analyze_span. If any pair has both observation and claim as None, that pair raises ConsistencyAnalysisError. Processing continues for valid pairs; the batch call is not atomic (partial results are not returned on error — the error propagates).

inputs.name: pairs
inputs.description: Sequence of (observation, claim) pairs. Each pair must have at least one non-None element.

error_data.detail: analyze_batch called with empty pairs sequence

error_cases.name: pair_both_none
error_cases.condition: Any pair has both observation and claim as None

error_data.detail: Pair at index {i} has both observation and claim as None

error_cases.name: pair_id_mismatch
error_cases.condition: Any pair where both are non-None has mismatched span_id, node_id, or trace_id

error_data.detail: ID mismatch in pair at index {i}: {details}

preconditions: For each pair, at least one of observation or claim is non-None
For each pair where both are non-None, span_id, node_id, and trace_id must match
postconditions: len(result) == len(pairs)
result[i] corresponds to pairs[i] for all valid indices
Each result element satisfies all postconditions of analyze_span
Results are ordered to match input pair ordering

structured_side_effects.description: Pure computation; no side effects. Caller is responsible for persisting returned findings.

performance_budget.complexity: O(m * n) where m = number of pairs, n = max fields per pair

functions.name: persist
functions.description: Appends a single ConsistencyFinding to the finding store. The store is append-only; findings are never updated or deleted. Serialization uses JSONL format with sorted field arrays and ISO 8601 UTC timestamps. This is the only write path into the finding store.

error_cases.name: io_error
error_cases.condition: Underlying storage is unavailable or write fails

error_data.detail: Failed to persist finding for node {node_id}, span {span_id}: {io_error}

error_cases.name: serialization_error
error_cases.condition: Finding cannot be serialized to JSONL format

error_data.detail: Serialization failed for finding node {node_id}, span {span_id}: {ser_error}

preconditions: finding is a valid ConsistencyFinding with all required fields populated
finding.analyzed_at is a valid UTC timestamp
postconditions: The finding is durably stored and will appear in subsequent get_by_node and get_by_span queries
The finding store size has increased by exactly one entry
If has_high_severity was False before and finding.severity == HIGH, has_high_severity returns True after

structured_side_effects.description: Appends one JSONL line to the findings store file. Append-only, no updates or deletes.
Logs finding persist at DEBUG level with node_id and span_id.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n = total fields in the finding (for serialization)

functions.name: get_by_node
functions.description: Retrieves all persisted ConsistencyFindings for a given node_id, ordered chronologically by analyzed_at (oldest first). Used by trust engine to compute consistency_factor and by CLI `arbiter findings` for per-node queries.

inputs.name: node_id
inputs.description: The node identifier to query findings for.

error_cases.name: io_error
error_cases.condition: Underlying storage is unavailable or read fails

error_data.detail: Failed to read findings for node {node_id}: {io_error}

error_cases.name: deserialization_error
error_cases.condition: Stored finding data is corrupt or has incompatible schema_version

error_data.detail: Deserialization failed for finding in node {node_id} store: {deser_error}

postconditions: All returned findings have finding.node_id == node_id
Results are ordered by analyzed_at ascending (oldest first)
Returns empty list if no findings exist for the given node_id

structured_side_effects.description: Reads from the findings store file to retrieve findings matching the given node_id.

performance_budget.complexity: O(N) where N = total findings in store (full scan; index-backed implementations may be O(k) where k = matching findings)

functions.name: get_by_span
functions.description: Retrieves all persisted ConsistencyFindings for a given span_id, ordered chronologically by analyzed_at (oldest first). Typically returns zero or one finding per span, but the list type accommodates re-analysis scenarios.

inputs.name: span_id
inputs.description: The span identifier to query findings for.

error_cases.name: io_error
error_cases.condition: Underlying storage is unavailable or read fails

error_data.detail: Failed to read findings for span {span_id}: {io_error}

error_cases.name: deserialization_error
error_cases.condition: Stored finding data is corrupt or has incompatible schema_version

error_data.detail: Deserialization failed for finding in span {span_id} store: {deser_error}

postconditions: All returned findings have finding.span_id == span_id
Results are ordered by analyzed_at ascending (oldest first)
Returns empty list if no findings exist for the given span_id

structured_side_effects.description: Reads from the findings store file to retrieve findings matching the given span_id.

performance_budget.complexity: O(N) where N = total findings in store (full scan; index-backed implementations may be O(k) where k = matching findings)

functions.name: has_high_severity
functions.description: Checks whether any HIGH severity ConsistencyFinding exists for the given node_id, optionally filtered to findings analyzed at or after a given timestamp. Used by the soak policy layer to enforce hard-stop on HIGH severity inconsistencies during soak. Returns False when no findings exist for the node.

inputs.name: node_id
inputs.description: The node identifier to check for HIGH severity findings.

inputs.name: since
inputs.description: Optional UTC timestamp lower bound. If provided, only findings with analyzed_at >= since are considered. If None, all findings for the node are considered.

error_cases.name: io_error
error_cases.condition: Underlying storage is unavailable or read fails

error_data.detail: Failed to check high severity for node {node_id}: {io_error}

preconditions: If since is provided, it is a valid UTC timestamp
postconditions: Returns True iff at least one ConsistencyFinding exists with finding.node_id == node_id AND finding.severity == HIGH AND (since is None OR finding.analyzed_at >= since)
Returns False if no findings exist for the given node_id
Returns False if findings exist but none have HIGH severity in the time range

structured_side_effects.description: Reads from the findings store to check for HIGH severity findings matching criteria.

performance_budget.complexity: O(N) where N = total findings for the node (can short-circuit on first HIGH match)

invariants: Trust is earned (computed from ledger), authority is declared (from manifests) — never conflated
Adapter observations are ground truth; node self-reports are claims
ConsistencyFinding.outcome == CONSISTENT iff unexplained_fields is empty AND overclaimed_fields is empty AND both observation and claim are present
ConsistencyFinding.outcome == MISSING_CLAIM when observation is present but claim is None
ConsistencyFinding.outcome == MISSING_OBSERVATION when claim is present but observation is None
All timestamps are UTC (datetime with timezone.utc)
Finding severity is computed deterministically from outcome and unexplained field count — never from display tiers
FindingStore is append-only for persist operations — no updates, no deletes
analyze_span with both observation=None and claim=None is invalid and raises ConsistencyAnalysisError
analyze_span with empty observed_fields and empty claimed_fields (both present) produces CONSISTENT with NONE severity (vacuous truth)
schema_version on ConsistencyFinding is always 1 for this contract version
get_by_node and get_by_span return findings in chronological order (oldest first) by analyzed_at
has_high_severity returns False when no findings exist for the given node_id
Error messages always include the specific node_id, span_id, or field that caused the error
